documentation.description:
If a procedure sets Status to Error, the record returned by function
State will provide more information useful for debugging.

record_types.name: I2C_Abort_Source

fields.name: No_Ack_Transmit
fields.description: Upon controller transmit, the address was acknowledged, but a data byte was not.

fields.name: No_Ack_General_Call
fields.description: No target acknowledged a general call

fields.name: Invalid_General_Call
fields.description: General call command with read bit set

fields.name: Invalid_Ack_High_Speed
fields.description: High speed mode was selected, but an ack was received before the end of the transfer.

fields.name: Invalid_Ack_Start
fields.description: A start byte was acknowledged, but only data should be acknowledged.

fields.name: No_Restart_High_Speed
fields.description: High speed mode was selected but restart is not enabled

fields.name: No_Restart_Start
fields.description: Attempting to send a start byte but restart is not enabled

fields.name: No_Restart_10b_Read
fields.description: 10 bit read attempted but restart is not enabled

fields.name: Not_Controller_Mode
fields.description: A controller operation was attempted while in target mode

fields.name: Arbitration_Lost
fields.description: Something pulled SDA low before being addressed by the controller

fields.name: Target_Transmit_Abort
fields.description:
A target read command was issued while there was still data in the
transmit FIFO, transmit aborted.

fields.name: Target_Arbitration_Lost
fields.description: Something pulled SDA to the wrong state while transmitting in target mode

fields.name: Invalid_Target_Read
fields.description: The read bit was set in the CMD register during a target transmit

fields.name: Transfer_Aborted
fields.description: User aborted a transfer in controller mode

fields.name: Transmit_Flush_Count
fields.description: Number of times the transmit FIFO was flushed due to an abort

record_types.name: I2C_Config
I2C_State
I2C_Timing

tagged_types.name: I2C_Port

constants.name: Fast_Mode
constants.code:
Fast_Mode      : constant I2C_Timing :=
   (High   => 1_160,
    Low    => 1_330,
    Hold   => 800,
    Spike  => 50,
    others => <>);

constants.name: Fast_Mode_Plus
constants.code:
Fast_Mode_Plus : constant I2C_Timing :=
   (High   => 400,
    Low    => 600,
    Hold   => 200,
    Spike  => 50,
    others => <>);

constants.name: Standard_Mode
constants.code:
Standard_Mode  : constant I2C_Timing :=
   (High   => 5_200,
    Low    => 4_700,
    Hold   => 3_450,
    others => <>);

subprograms.name: Configure
Disable
Enable
Read

documentation.description: Returns True if a call to Read would not block

subprograms.name: Set_Address

documentation.description:
When configured as a Controller, Set_Address indicates the address of the Target for Read and Write.
When configured as a Target, Set_Address indicates this device's address.

subprograms.name: Set_Address
Set_Timing
Start_Read

documentation.description:
If Stop is False, the Controller does not release the bus after this
transaction completes and will issue a repeated start before the first
byte of the next transaction. Stop has no effect if Role = Target.

subprograms.name: Start_Write

documentation.description:
In Target mode, Start_Write waits until Deadline for the Controller to
send our address. If a Deadline is specified and nonblocking behavior is
expected, the caller should check the value of Write_Ready before
calling Write.

subprograms.name: Write

documentation.description:
If Deadline is reached before Write_Ready returns True, Abort_Write will
be called and Status is set to Err_Timeout. After a timeout, call
Clear_Error before attempting another Write.
Returns True if a call to Write would not block
